title: fix(server): allow thread ownership transfer in trusted server contexts
summary: Allows thread ownership transfer (resourceId reassignment) in trusted server contexts where no middleware sets MASTRA_RESOURCE_ID_KEY. The core change is a ~15-line modification in packages/server/src/server/handlers/memory.ts: the UPDATE_THREAD_ROUTE handler now reads contextResourceId directly from middleware context instead of using getEffectiveResourceId (which merged middleware and client values). When middleware is present, resourceId remains locked; without middleware, the client-provided resourceId is accepted for transfer. The bulk of the diff (570+ lines) is comprehensive tests covering ownership lock with middleware, transfer without middleware, working memory scoping (resource and thread scope), observational memory isolation, and message access/denial after transfer. Documentation updated across 4 files: middleware docs, client-js reference, server routes reference, and course material.
maintainerNotes: Well-structured PR from a maintainer. The actual code change is minimal and surgical (~15 lines in memory.ts), while the test coverage is thorough with 10+ test cases covering edge cases like memory scoping isolation after transfer. CI failures (Agent Builder, Core, MCP, Observability) appear unrelated to this PR — those packages are untouched and the failures show 0 duration suggesting infrastructure/flaky issues. Build, lint, and validation checks all pass. E2E and Memory tests are still pending. The security model is sound: middleware-enforced resourceId prevents transfer from untrusted clients, while trusted server code without middleware can reassign. Closes issue #13327 which is labeled impact:high. No similar or duplicate PRs found.
